results.title: Prometheus by Firecrawl | aat.ee
results.content:
### Worth checking out if…

You regularly need web data but find yourself spending more time maintaining scrapers than analyzing results. Prometheus is especially valuable if you're prototyping data collection ideas quickly, or if you need reliable, ongoing extraction from pages that change frequently. It's also worth exploring if you want to hand off data collection to an automated system while keeping the codebase accessible for customization.

#Developer Tools#Artificial Intelligence [...] ### What is Prometheus by Firecrawl?

Prometheus is an experimental Forward Deployed Agent that transforms plain-English data requests into working Firecrawl collectors. Named after the Titan who gifted fire to humanity, this tool bridges the gap between natural language and structured web data extraction. You describe what data you need in everyday language, and Prometheus writes the Firecrawl SDK code to collect it. The result is a reproducible, versionable collector plus the sample data it produced — entirely yours to keep, modify, or deploy.

### Who it's for [...] ### Who it's for

 Data analysts and researchers who need specific web data but lack the time or coding expertise to build custom scrapers from scratch
 Developers building data pipelines who want to rapidly prototype collectors and then hand them off for scheduled, maintained execution
 Product teams monitoring competitors or market trends who need ongoing, reliable data collection without constant manual intervention

### Key features

#### Plain-English data requests

You describe the web data you need in natural language — no complex configuration or scripting required. Prometheus interprets your request and generates the appropriate Firecrawl code to collect exactly what you specified.

#### Reproducible, versionable collectors

As a forward deployed agent, Prometheus is engineered to be embedded within various environments, enabling it to interact with existing software, APIs, and platforms to deliver intelligent assistance tailored to specific organizational needs. results.content:
# Firecrawl Introduces Prometheus: A Forward-Deployed Agent for Web Data : r/AIinBusinessNews
Skip to main contentFirecrawl Introduces Prometheus: A Forward-Deployed Agent for Web Data : r/AIinBusinessNews

Open menu Open navigation, aims to fix that. Instead of writing custom code or fighting with shifting CSS selectors, you just tell it what data you want in plain English (e.g., _"give me the top 5 stories on Hacker News"_).

How it works:

   Build: It drives a headless browser, figures out the site layout, writes a TypeScript script, tests it in a sandbox, and hands you the working code. [...] Script & Self-Heal: If you host it with Firecrawl and the target website changes its layout, Prometheus automatically re-analyzes the new DOM, rewrites the code, and updates the version history—meaning zero manual maintenance for broken scrapers.

   Deploy: You can trigger it via an API or set it up on a continuous Cron schedule.

# Integrate OceanBase MCP Server with TRAE

folded

Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open-source protocol introduced by Anthropic in November 2024. It allows large language models to interact with external tools or data sources. With MCP, you do not need to manually copy and execute the output of large language models. Instead, the large language model can directly command tools to perform specific actions. [...] You have created a MySQL-compatible tenant in the instance. For instructions on how to create the tenant, see Create a MySQL-compatible tenant.

You have a MySQL database and account available under the tenant, and you have granted read and write permissions to the database account. For more information, see Create an account and Create a database (MySQL only).

You are a project admin or instance admin and have the permissions required to read and write data in the instance. If not, contact your organization admin to grant the required permissions.

You have installed Python 3.11 or later and pip. If your local machine has an earlier version of Python, you can use Miniconda to create a new Python 3.11 or later environment. For more information, see Miniconda installation guide. [...] You can see that the RESTful APIs for CRUD operations have been successfully generated:
